title: Virgin Galactic (SPCE) Q3 2025 Earnings Transcript
text: Virgin Galactic reported Q3 2025 results showing meaningful progress toward commercial spaceflight operations. The company generated $400,000 in revenue from astronaut access fees while reducing operating expenses by 19% year-over-year to $67 million. The enterprise achieved a net loss of $64 million, representing a 15% improvement compared to the prior year. Free cash flow stood at negative $108 million, an 8% improvement despite increased capital investments. Cash reserves totaled $424 million at quarter-end. Management highlighted critical manufacturing milestones, particularly the qualification of a next-generation oxidizer tank for 500 or more space flights compared to the previous 40-flight limit. The company expects approximately 90% of carbon and metallic components for the first spaceship to arrive by mid-December. Leadership reaffirmed plans for flight testing to commence in Q3 2026, with commercial service beginning in Q4 2026. CEO Michael Colglazier stated ticket prices for future sales will be higher than our last published price, which was $600,000. With two operational spaceships, Virgin Galactic projects approximately $450 million in annual revenue and $100 million in adjusted EBITDA at steady-state operations, scaling to approximately $1 billion revenue and $500 million EBITDA with fleet expansion.
